---
title: "Patient-robust discovery of combinatorial cell-surface targets in prostate cancer"
subtitle: "AND-gate and NOT-gate marker pairs from single-cell data, benchmarked against PSMA-PSCA"
author: "William Guesdon"
date: now
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm"
abstract: |
  Single-antigen therapies against solid tumors are limited by on-target off-tumor toxicity, because
  few surface antigens are truly absent from all healthy tissue. Combinatorial (logic-gated) targeting
  requires two conditions before a cell is engaged, which can recover specificity. We scan a curated
  cell-surface panel in single-cell data for pairs that discriminate prostate cancer cells from healthy
  human cell types, under AND (both markers on the tumor) and NOT (an activator spared by a blocker on
  healthy cells) logic. Pairs are scored per patient and summarized across patients, so no high
  cell-count patient drives a result. The preclinically validated PSMA-PSCA balanced-signalling pair is
  recovered as a positive control: each marker alone carries a large extra-prostatic transcript liability,
  and the AND gate collapses it. We report a Pareto frontier of surface-accessible pairs with higher
  per-patient malignant co-detection than PSMA-PSCA at comparable predicted normal-tissue liability, with
  Human Protein Atlas evidence supporting membrane-localization plausibility. These are transcript-level,
  hypothesis-generating rankings, not evidence of therapeutic safety.
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    css: styles/report.css
    title-block-banner: false
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-title: "Contents"
    toc-location: left
    number-sections: true
    code-fold: true
    embed-resources: true
    fig-align: center
    fig-width: 8
    fig-height: 5
  typst:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
    fig-align: center
bibliography: references.bib
engine: jupyter
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  cache: false
---

```{python}
#| tags: [setup]
from pathlib import Path
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib as mpl
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from IPython.display import Markdown, display

REPO = Path.cwd().parent if Path.cwd().name == "reports" else Path.cwd()
T = REPO / "results" / "tables"

def load(name):
    p = T / name
    return pd.read_csv(p) if p.exists() else None

manifest   = load("data_manifest.csv")
summary    = load("analysis_summary.csv").iloc[0]
panel      = load("surface_panel.csv")
singles    = load("singles_markers.csv")
pairs_and  = load("pairs_and.csv")
frontier   = load("surface_frontier.csv")
recovery   = load("psma_psca_recovery.csv")
negctrl    = load("negative_control.csv")
protein    = load("protein_evidence.csv")
nomination = load("nomination.csv").iloc[0]
nom_not    = load("nomination_not.csv")
sens       = load("threshold_sensitivity.csv")   # may be absent early
perpat     = load("nomination_per_patient.csv")
tissue_liab= load("pair_tissue_liability.csv")
dbl_rm     = load("doublet_removal.csv")
dbl_qc     = load("doublet_qc.csv")
unc_cov    = load("uncertainty_coverage.csv")
unc_liab   = load("uncertainty_liability.csv")
unc_paired = load("uncertainty_paired.csv")
deleaked   = load("deleaked_sensitivity.csv")

# Headline scalars, all bound to committed tables.
N_TUMOR   = int(manifest.loc[manifest.dataset == "tumor_localised_pca", "n_cells"].iloc[0])
N_DONOR   = int(manifest.loc[manifest.dataset == "tumor_localised_pca", "n_donors"].iloc[0])
N_MAL     = int(summary.n_malignant_cells)
N_PAT     = int(summary.n_patients_scored)
N_BEN     = int(summary.n_benign_cells)
N_HEALTHY = int(summary.n_healthy_cells)
N_CT      = int(summary.n_healthy_celltypes)
N_TIS     = int(summary.n_healthy_tissues)
N_POP     = int(summary.n_healthy_populations)
N_GENE    = int(summary.n_panel_genes_scanned)
K         = int(summary.k_threshold)

# Report palette and matplotlib defaults.
PALETTE = {"ink": "#0A0F29", "blue": "#1E4FA3", "blue2": "#2E6FD0", "faint": "#dce5f2",
           "vermillion": "#c9542b", "grey": "#8c8c8c", "grid": "#e6e6e6", "green": "#2e7d5b"}
plt.rcParams.update({
    "figure.dpi": 130, "savefig.dpi": 130, "font.size": 10, "font.family": "sans-serif",
    "axes.edgecolor": "#b8b8b2", "axes.linewidth": 0.8, "axes.grid": True,
    "grid.color": PALETTE["grid"], "grid.linewidth": 0.7, "axes.axisbelow": True,
    "axes.spines.top": False, "axes.spines.right": False,
})

def pair_row(df, a, b, ca="marker_a", cb="marker_b"):
    m = (((df[ca] == a) & (df[cb] == b)) | ((df[ca] == b) & (df[cb] == a)))
    return df[m].iloc[0]
```

# Background

A major challenge for antigen-directed therapies in solid tumors is achieving tumor coverage without
clinically important on-target, off-tumor activity. A single tumor antigen is rarely absent from every
healthy tissue, so single-target CAR-T, T-cell engagers, and antibody-drug conjugates carry this risk. Combinatorial, logic-gated targeting requires two
conditions before a cell is engaged and can recover the missing specificity [@kloss2013combinatorial;
@roybal2016precision; @cho2018universal]. Prostate adenocarcinoma is the natural test bed: the
PSMA (FOLH1) x PSCA pair is a preclinically validated AND-gate split-signal CAR [@kloss2013combinatorial],
so a systematic scan of a curated panel can be checked against a known answer, and STEAP1 anchors a
second axis with a STEAP1 x CD3 T-cell engager (xaluritamig) in trials [@kelly2024xaluritamig]. That
trial establishes STEAP1 as druggable in prostate cancer; it does not validate a PSMA x STEAP1 gate.

Computational logic-gated antigen discovery is not itself new: pan-cancer single-cell pair scans
[@kwon2023singlecell] and a breast-cancer AND/OR/NOT circuit search [@madan2025logicar] precede this
work. Our contribution is the combination of (i) per-patient scoring rather than pooled cells,
(ii) a matched benign-prostate control that tests tumor specificity in the same samples, (iii) an
assay-matched, donor-robust healthy reference so normal-tissue liabilities are comparable across
platforms and not driven by a single donor, and (iv) recovery of the PSMA-PSCA benchmark before any new
pair is nominated. The nominated target pair is not itself novel: a dual PSMA/STEAP1 antibody-drug
conjugate (ABBV-969) is in phase 1 [@abbv969], though as an OR-like agent that binds either antigen rather
than a strict AND gate.

# Data

```{python}
Markdown(f"""
- **Tumor discovery cohort** — {N_TUMOR:,} cells from **{N_DONOR} patients** with localised, hormone
  therapy-naive prostate adenocarcinoma [@localisedpca2024]. Malignant identity uses the authors'
  copy-number and signature-based annotation: **{N_MAL:,} malignant cells** (scDblFinder singlets;
  see Methods), with **{N_BEN:,} adjacent-benign / normal prostate epithelial cells** retained as a
  matched same-patient control.
- **Healthy reference** — Tabula Sapiens 2.0 [@tabulasapiens2024; @tabulasapiens2022], pulled through the
  CELLxGENE Census (release 2025-01-30) and restricted to the **10x 3' v3** assay to match the tumor
  cohort chemistry, since a raw-count positivity threshold is not comparable across assays: **{N_HEALTHY:,}
  cells**, **{N_CT} cell types** across **{N_TIS} tissue labels** (about 30 organs); **{N_POP}
  donor-replicated tissue-by-cell-type populations** (at least 2 donors each) form the safety denominator.
- **Protein evidence** — Human Protein Atlas subcellular localization and normal-tissue RNA
  [@uhlen2015hpa], used to qualify surface accessibility and cross-check normal-tissue liabilities.
- **Panel** — {N_GENE} curated cell-surface markers scanned (see Methods); secreted markers (KLK3/PSA,
  KLK2, ACPP) are kept only as comparators.
""")
```

# Methods

```{python}
if dbl_rm is not None:
    d = dbl_rm.iloc[0]
    maxd = float(np.abs(dbl_qc["delta"]).max()) if dbl_qc is not None else float("nan")
    display(Markdown(
        f"- **Doublet removal (primary input).** scDblFinder, run per patient in a Bioconductor "
        f"container, flagged **{d['doublet_rate']*100:.1f}%** of tumor cells as doublets "
        f"({d['malignant_doublet_rate']*100:.1f}% of malignant cells); these are removed before "
        f"scoring, so a two-cells-as-one capture cannot fake AND co-expression (Rule 8). Re-scoring "
        f"on the {int(d['n_cells_singlet']):,} retained single cells moves the key pairs' AND coverage "
        f"by at most {maxd*100:.1f} percentage points, so the finding is not a doublet artifact."
    ))
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-doublet-qc
#| fig-cap: "Doublet robustness. Median per-patient AND coverage of the key pairs computed on all malignant cells (grey) versus the scDblFinder singlets actually used for scoring (blue). The bars are near-identical and the change (annotated, percentage points) is at most one point, so the co-expression signal is not an artifact of two cells captured as one; removing doublets nudges the nominated pairs slightly upward."
if dbl_qc is not None:
    q = dbl_qc.reset_index(drop=True)
    labels = [p.replace("FOLH1", "PSMA").replace("+", " x ") for p in q["pair"]]
    x = np.arange(len(q)); w = 0.38
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 3.4))
    ax.bar(x - w/2, q["and_coverage_all"], width=w, color=PALETTE["grey"], label="all malignant cells")
    ax.bar(x + w/2, q["and_coverage_singlets"], width=w, color=PALETTE["blue"], label="singlets (scored)")
    for i, r in q.iterrows():
        top = max(r["and_coverage_all"], r["and_coverage_singlets"])
        ax.text(i, top + 0.02, f"{r['delta']*100:+.1f} pp", ha="center", fontsize=8, color=PALETTE["ink"])
    ax.set_xticks(x); ax.set_xticklabels(labels); ax.set_ylim(0, 1)
    ax.set_ylabel("AND coverage (median across patients)")
    ax.legend(frameon=False, fontsize=8, loc="upper left")
    plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
```

- **Positivity** is a per-cell raw count of at least `{python} K` (single-cell detection, not a threshold
  on a pooled or averaged expression value); the threshold is swept for sensitivity (below).
- **Per-patient scoring (never pooled).** For markers A, B and each patient's malignant cells,
  AND coverage is `P(A+ and B+ | malignant)` computed within that patient, then summarized across
  patients by the lower decile (Q0.10, the robustness floor) and the median. A patient with thousands of
  cells cannot dominate a pair's score.
- **Three references per pair.** (1) malignant coverage; (2) **matched benign prostate** from the same
  cohort, so a pair expressed across all prostate cells is penalised, not just one specific to the tumor;
  (3) **healthy liability** as the worst-case co-positive fraction over Tabula Sapiens populations. The
  healthy side is assay-matched to the tumor (10x 3' v3 only) and made **donor-robust**: within each
  tissue-by-cell-type population the fraction is computed per donor and summarized by the median across
  donors, keeping only populations with at least two donors, so no single donor or tiny population sets a
  worst case. Every worst-liability call carries its supporting donor and cell counts. Liabilities are
  reported both across all organs (the stricter statistic, retained in the tables) and **excluding the
  prostate** (normal-prostate expression may be clinically tolerable in selected advanced-disease
  contexts, whereas every other organ is dose-limiting); the figures use the extra-prostatic statistic.
- **Label-leakage control.** The cohort's malignant label is built from the Liu and Wallace
  prostate-cancer expression signatures plus CopyKAT aneuploidy. HPN is a member of both signatures and
  EPCAM of the Liu signature, so any pair containing HPN or EPCAM is confounded: the gene helps define the
  label it then scores against. Such pairs are flagged, held off the Pareto frontier, and not nominated;
  the nominated PSMA x STEAP1 lead and the PSMA-PSCA control contain no signature gene. A full fix
  recomputes the labels with all 29 screened genes removed from the signatures (future work).
- **Gate algebra.** For each group a single matrix product over the float32 panel positivity matrix
  yields AND, NOT (`A+ and B-`) and OR counts for every pair at once; co-positive counts stay far below
  the float32 exact-integer limit, so there is no overflow, and NOT gates are directed (all 812
  activator-blocker orientations are scored). These Boolean gates are screening abstractions of candidate
  receptor logic; they do not model antigen-density thresholds, residual single-arm activity, or the
  architecture-specific signalling of a balanced split-signal CAR, synNotch, or Tmod circuit.
- **Ranking.** Pairs are placed on a Pareto frontier of per-patient coverage floor (higher better)
  against worst extra-prostatic liability (lower better). Only surface-accessible markers are
  nominatable.
- **Reproducibility.** Every number below is read from a committed table in `results/tables/`; the report
  redraws its figures from those tables with no network or model call. The healthy pull uses the Census
  raw-count layer with `is_primary_data == True` (deduplicated cells), release `2025-01-30`, and confirms
  the panel features are present in the query (ACPP was the one panel gene absent).

# Results

## Single surface antigens each carry a large normal-tissue liability

```{python}
#| label: fig-singles
#| fig-cap: "Single surface antigens: per-patient malignant coverage (median) against the worst-case extra-prostatic normal-tissue co-detection. Every strong single antigen also carries a large normal-tissue transcript liability; the recovered worst tissues are concordant with known biology (FOLH1/PSMA to duodenum, PSCA to bladder urothelium)."
keys = ["FOLH1","PSCA","STEAP1","STEAP2","STEAP4","TMPRSS2","HPN","TACSTD2","CD276","DLL3"]
s = singles.set_index("marker").loc[[k for k in keys if k in singles.marker.values]]
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 4.2))
y = np.arange(len(s))
ax.barh(y-0.2, s["tumor_median"], height=0.4, color=PALETTE["blue"], label="malignant coverage (median)")
ax.barh(y+0.2, s["worst_healthy_xprostate"], height=0.4, color=PALETTE["vermillion"], label="worst extra-prostatic liability")
for i,(m,r) in enumerate(s.iterrows()):
    ax.text(r["worst_healthy_xprostate"]+0.01, i+0.2, str(r["worst_xp_celltype"])[:26], va="center", fontsize=7, color=PALETTE["grey"])
ax.set_yticks(y); ax.set_yticklabels([f"{m} ({s.loc[m,'alt_name']})" if 'alt_name' in s.columns else m for m in s.index])
ax.invert_yaxis(); ax.set_xlim(0,1.15); ax.set_xlabel("fraction of cells positive"); ax.legend(loc="lower right", fontsize=8, frameon=False)
plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
```

- Each strong single antigen is broadly positive on the tumor but also on at least one healthy cell
  type: the scan independently recovers PSMA's duodenal and PSCA's bladder-urothelial expression, a
  qualitative sanity check that the recovered tissues match known biology, not a claim that the fractions
  are quantitatively exact.

## The AND gate recovers PSMA-PSCA and collapses its single-agent liabilities

```{python}
#| label: fig-recovery
#| fig-cap: "Positive control. PSMA (FOLH1) and PSCA are each broadly detected off the prostate (duodenum, bladder), but requiring both at once (AND) collapses the worst extra-prostatic co-detection roughly sixfold, recapitulating the direction of the preclinical rationale for the split-signal PSMA x PSCA CAR."
r = recovery.set_index("entity")
order = list(r.index)
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 3.6))
colors = [PALETTE["vermillion"], PALETTE["vermillion"], PALETTE["green"]]
bars = ax.bar(order, r["worst_healthy_xprostate"], color=colors, width=0.6)
for b,(e,row) in zip(bars, r.iterrows()):
    ax.text(b.get_x()+b.get_width()/2, row["worst_healthy_xprostate"]+0.02, f"{row['worst_healthy_xprostate']:.2f}\n{str(row['worst_xp_celltype'])[:18]}", ha="center", va="bottom", fontsize=7.5)
ax.set_ylim(0,1.05); ax.set_ylabel("worst extra-prostatic liability"); ax.set_xlabel("")
plt.xticks(rotation=8); plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
```

```{python}
pc = pair_row(pairs_and, "FOLH1", "PSCA")
sa = singles.set_index("marker")
red = max(sa.loc["FOLH1","worst_healthy_xprostate"], sa.loc["PSCA","worst_healthy_xprostate"]) - pc["worst_healthy_xprostate"]
frac_worse = float(negctrl.loc[negctrl.row=="summary","frac_random_pairs_worse_than_psma_psca"].iloc[0])
Markdown(f"""
- **Recovered.** PSMA alone reaches **{sa.loc['FOLH1','worst_healthy_xprostate']:.2f}** co-detection in
  duodenal enterocytes and PSCA alone **{sa.loc['PSCA','worst_healthy_xprostate']:.2f}** in bladder
  urothelium; the **PSMA and PSCA** gate drops the worst extra-prostatic co-detection to
  **{pc['worst_healthy_xprostate']:.2f}** (a reduction of {red:.2f}), the expected direction of the
  preclinical benchmark.
- **Against a random baseline.** {frac_worse*100:.0f}% of the sampled surface pairs (drawn from the same
  curated panel) score worse than PSMA-PSCA on extra-prostatic selectivity, defined as median malignant
  co-detection minus worst extra-prostatic liability. This is a weak signal: a pair can also look clean by
  carrying little tumor coverage, so it is a sanity check, not proof of specificity.
- Its low malignant co-detection ({pc['tumor_median']:.2f} median) is consistent with limited PSCA
  transcript detection in this dataset; protein-level measurement would be needed to separate technical
  non-detection from biological heterogeneity. It motivates looking for higher-coverage pairs of
  comparable liability.
""")
```

## A Pareto frontier of surface pairs with higher coverage than PSMA-PSCA

```{python}
#| label: fig-pareto
#| fig-cap: "Surface-accessible AND pairs (label-leakage pairs excluded): per-patient coverage floor (Q0.10) against worst extra-prostatic co-detection. Upper-left is better. Frontier pairs are filled; the PSMA-PSCA control and the nominated PSMA x STEAP1 lead are labelled. Excluded as non-nominatable: secreted markers, intracellular prostein, and any pair containing a malignant-label signature gene (HPN, EPCAM)."
f = frontier[~frontier["leak"]].copy()
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7.5, 5))
off = f[~f.on_frontier]; on = f[f.on_frontier]
ax.scatter(off["worst_healthy_xprostate"], off["tumor_q10"], s=22, color=PALETTE["grey"], alpha=0.5, label="surface pair")
ax.scatter(on["worst_healthy_xprostate"], on["tumor_q10"], s=48, color=PALETTE["blue"], label="Pareto frontier", zorder=3)
labels = {("FOLH1","PSCA"):"PSMA x PSCA (control)", ("FOLH1","STEAP1"):"PSMA x STEAP1 (lead)"}
for (a,b),txt in labels.items():
    row = pair_row(f, a, b)
    ax.scatter(row["worst_healthy_xprostate"], row["tumor_q10"], s=70, facecolor="none", edgecolor=PALETTE["vermillion"], linewidth=1.6, zorder=4)
    ax.annotate(txt, (row["worst_healthy_xprostate"], row["tumor_q10"]), textcoords="offset points", xytext=(8,4), fontsize=8.5, color=PALETTE["ink"])
ax.set_xlabel("worst extra-prostatic co-detection  (lower is better)")
ax.set_ylabel("per-patient coverage floor  Q0.10  (higher is better)")
ax.set_xlim(-0.02, max(0.6, f["worst_healthy_xprostate"].max()*1.05)); ax.set_ylim(-0.02, 1.0)
ax.legend(loc="upper right", fontsize=8, frameon=False); plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
```

```{python}
fold = float(nomination["coverage_fold_vs_psma_psca"])
pc2 = pair_row(pairs_and, "FOLH1", "PSCA")
Markdown(f"""
One surface-accessible pair is nominated. It is clean of label leakage (neither marker is a
malignant-label signature gene) and, once the leakage-confounded HPN pairs are removed, it is
Pareto-non-dominated among the surface-accessible candidates.

- **PSMA x STEAP1 — nominated lead.** Both antigens have clinical-grade binders. Per-patient coverage
  floor **Q0.10 = {nomination['tumor_q10']:.2f}**, median **{nomination['tumor_median']:.2f}**
  (**{fold:.1f}x** the PSMA-PSCA median). Its worst extra-prostatic co-detection is
  **{nomination['worst_healthy_xprostate']:.2f}** in {nomination['worst_xp_celltype']}
  ({int(nomination['worst_xp_n_donors'])} donors), versus {nomination['single_a_worst_xp']:.2f} /
  {nomination['single_b_worst_xp']:.2f} for the single antigens and {pc2['worst_healthy_xprostate']:.2f}
  for PSMA-PSCA. Whether that residual liability is clinically acceptable cannot be judged from transcript
  data. Median coverage {nomination['tumor_median']:.2f} and floor {nomination['tumor_q10']:.2f} are not
  obviously adequate for monotherapy; a sizeable tumor fraction can remain double-negative or
  single-positive.
- **Higher in malignant than adjacent-benign prostate.** The pair shows more co-detection in annotated
  malignant than in matched benign prostate epithelium (malignant-minus-benign
  {nomination['malignant_vs_benign']:.2f}).
- **The pair is not novel, and AND is not the only architecture.** A dual PSMA/STEAP1 antibody-drug
  conjugate (ABBV-969) is in phase 1 [@abbv969], but as a single molecule binding either antigen it is
  OR-like, not a strict AND gate. Our contribution is the per-patient, benchmark-anchored AND framing,
  not the target pair.
- **HPN pairs are excluded as confounded.** STEAP1 x HPN and PSMA x HPN scored well, but HPN is a member
  of the Liu and Wallace signatures used to build the malignant-cell label, so their coverage is inflated
  by label leakage (see Methods); they are not nominated pending recomputation of the labels without the
  screened genes.
""")
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-and-candidates
cand = frontier[~frontier["leak"]].copy()  # surface_frontier.csv, label-leakage pairs removed
n_clean = len(cand); n_leak = int(frontier["leak"].sum())
cand["sel"] = cand["tumor_median"] - cand["worst_healthy_xprostate"]
cand = cand.sort_values("sel", ascending=False).head(15).reset_index(drop=True)
def _clab(a, b):
    star = " *" if {a, b} == {"FOLH1", "STEAP1"} else ""
    return f"{a.replace('FOLH1','PSMA')} x {b.replace('FOLH1','PSMA')}{star}"
cand["pair"] = [_clab(a, b) for a, b in zip(cand.marker_a, cand.marker_b)]
cand["frontier"] = np.where(cand["on_frontier"], "yes", "")
show = cand[["pair", "tumor_q10", "tumor_median", "worst_healthy_xprostate",
             "worst_xp_celltype", "worst_xp_n_donors", "malignant_vs_benign", "frontier"]].copy()
show.columns = ["pair", "Q0.10", "median", "worst xp", "worst cell type", "donors", "mal-ben", "frontier"]
for c in ["Q0.10", "median", "worst xp", "mal-ben"]:
    show[c] = show[c].round(2)
display(Markdown(
    f"**Surface-accessible AND candidates** (top 15 of {n_clean} clean targetable pairs; a further "
    f"{n_leak} pairs containing a malignant-label signature gene, HPN or EPCAM, are excluded as "
    "label-leakage confounded). Ranked by extra-prostatic selectivity: median malignant co-detection "
    "minus worst extra-prostatic co-detection. `*` marks the nominated lead; `frontier` marks "
    "Pareto-non-dominated pairs; `donors` supports the worst-liability population. Hypothesis-generating "
    "rankings over transcript co-detection, not validated targets.\n\n" + show.to_markdown(index=False)))
```

## Robustness: per patient and across positivity thresholds

```{python}
#| label: fig-perpatient
#| fig-cap: "Per-patient AND co-detection of the malignant compartment for the positive control, the nominated lead, and a clean high-coverage comparator. Each point is one patient; the bar is the median. PSMA x STEAP1 (lead) and STEAP1 x STEAP2 (clean, not nominated) hold a per-patient coverage floor (Q0.10) above 0.44 across scored patients, while PSMA x PSCA is uniformly low (limited PSCA transcript detection)."
pp = perpat.copy()
order = ["FOLH1 + PSCA", "FOLH1 + STEAP1", "STEAP1 + STEAP2"]
pp = pp[pp.pair.isin(order)]
colors = {"FOLH1 + PSCA": PALETTE["grey"], "FOLH1 + STEAP1": PALETTE["blue"], "STEAP1 + STEAP2": PALETTE["green"]}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7.5, 4))
rng = np.random.default_rng(1)
for i, name in enumerate(order):
    v = pp.loc[pp.pair == name, "coverage"].to_numpy()
    x = i + (rng.random(len(v)) - 0.5) * 0.28
    ax.scatter(x, v, s=26, color=colors[name], alpha=0.75, edgecolor="white", linewidth=0.4, zorder=3)
    ax.hlines(np.median(v), i-0.28, i+0.28, color=PALETTE["ink"], linewidth=2, zorder=4)
ax.set_xticks(range(len(order))); ax.set_xticklabels([o.replace("FOLH1","PSMA") for o in order])
ax.set_ylim(0, 1.0); ax.set_ylabel("per-patient malignant AND coverage")
plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-sensitivity
#| fig-cap: "Positivity-threshold sensitivity. As the per-cell call is tightened from >=1 to >=3 counts, malignant co-detection falls modestly while the worst extra-prostatic co-detection falls too: the candidates stay well covered and their normal-tissue liability drops, whereas PSMA x PSCA loses coverage. Positivity is a per-cell detection threshold, swept here, not a pooled-mean call."
if sens is not None:
    keys = ["FOLH1 + PSCA", "FOLH1 + STEAP1", "STEAP1 + STEAP2"]
    cmap = {"FOLH1 + PSCA": PALETTE["grey"], "FOLH1 + STEAP1": PALETTE["blue"], "STEAP1 + STEAP2": PALETTE["green"]}
    fig, (ax1, ax2) = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(9, 3.6))
    for name in keys:
        s = sens[sens.pair == name].sort_values("k")
        lab = name.replace("FOLH1","PSMA")
        ax1.plot(s.k, s.tumor_median, "-o", color=cmap[name], label=lab, markersize=5)
        ax2.plot(s.k, s.worst_healthy_xprostate, "-o", color=cmap[name], label=lab, markersize=5)
    ax1.set_xticks([1,2,3]); ax2.set_xticks([1,2,3])
    ax1.set_xlabel("positivity threshold (min counts)"); ax1.set_ylabel("malignant coverage (median)")
    ax2.set_xlabel("positivity threshold (min counts)"); ax2.set_ylabel("worst extra-prostatic liability")
    ax1.set_ylim(0,1); ax2.set_ylim(0, None); ax1.legend(fontsize=7.5, frameon=False)
    plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
else:
    display(Markdown("_Threshold-sensitivity table not yet generated._"))
```

- Both candidates survive threshold tightening: malignant co-detection decreases gradually while the
  worst extra-prostatic co-detection falls, so the selectivity ranking is not an artifact of the loosest
  positivity call.

```{python}
if tissue_liab is not None:
    tl = tissue_liab.copy()
    tl = tl[tl.tissue.str.lower() != "prostate gland"].head(6)
    display(Markdown("Residual off-tumor liabilities of PSMA x STEAP1 are named, not hidden. The highest "
             "extra-prostatic healthy populations (donor-robust AND co-detection, with donor and cell "
             "support):\n\n" +
             tl[["tissue","cell_type","coverage","n_donors","n_cells"]].round(3).to_markdown(index=False)))
```

```{python}
if unc_cov is not None and (unc_cov.pair == "PSMA x STEAP1").any():
    lead = unc_cov[unc_cov.pair == "PSMA x STEAP1"].iloc[0]
    liab = unc_liab.iloc[0]; pr = unc_paired.iloc[0]
    display(Markdown(f"""
**Uncertainty (bootstrap over patients; {int(lead['n_patients'])} patients, 2000 resamples).** Resampling
the unit of replication, patients rather than cells, gives between-subject intervals for PSMA x STEAP1:

- Median coverage **{lead['median']:.2f}** (95% CI {lead['median_lo']:.2f}--{lead['median_hi']:.2f}); the
  per-patient floor **Q0.10 = {lead['q10']:.2f}** is much less stable (95% CI
  {lead['q10_lo']:.2f}--{lead['q10_hi']:.2f}) and is reported as indicative, not precise. Coverage is at
  least 0.4 in {lead['frac_pat_ge_0.4']*100:.0f}% of patients.
- The pair stays **Pareto-non-dominated in {lead['lead_pareto_prob']*100:.1f}%** of patient resamples, so
  its frontier status is not an artifact of a few patients.
- Worst extra-prostatic liability ({liab['worst_pop']}): donor median **{liab['donor_median']:.2f}**, but
  the bootstrap **95% upper bound is {liab['donor_ub95']:.2f}** over {int(liab['n_donors'])} donors. A
  conservative safety read should use the upper bound, which is more than double the median.
- Malignant-minus-benign co-detection, paired within patient: median **{pr['median_delta']:.2f}**
  (95% CI {pr['delta_lo']:.2f}--{pr['delta_hi']:.2f}), positive in **{pr['frac_patients_positive']*100:.0f}%**
  of {int(pr['n_paired_patients'])} patients with both compartments, so the tumor enrichment is not a
  compositional average.
"""))
```

```{python}
if deleaked is not None and (deleaked.pair == "PSMA x STEAP1").any():
    d = deleaked.set_index("pair"); r = deleaked.iloc[0]
    display(Markdown(f"""
**De-leaked sensitivity (label leakage).** Recomputing the Liu and Wallace signatures with all 29 panel
genes removed barely changes them (Spearman **{r['sig_spearman_malignant']:.2f}** against the full
signature within malignant cells). A leakage-free malignant proxy, CopyKAT-aneuploid cells top-ranked by
the de-leaked signature and count-matched, recovers **{r['proxy_overlap_with_author']*100:.0f}%** of the
authors' malignant cells, and PSMA x STEAP1 coverage on it is essentially unchanged
(**{d.loc['PSMA x STEAP1','cov_deleaked_proxy']:.2f}** vs {d.loc['PSMA x STEAP1','cov_author_malignant']:.2f}).
The clean lead is not an artifact of the panel genes helping to define the compartment. This is a partial
control, not the authors' full reannotation.
"""))
```

## NOT gates: a negative result

```{python}
cols = ["activator","blocker","tumor_q10","tumor_median","worst_healthy_xprostate","worst_xp_celltype",
        "worst_xp_n_donors"]
nt = nom_not[cols].head(10).copy()
nt.columns = ["activator", "blocker", "Q0.10", "median", "worst xp", "worst cell type", "donors"]
for c in ["Q0.10", "median", "worst xp"]:
    nt[c] = nt[c].round(3)
Markdown("No NOT gate (activator on the tumor, blocker sparing a normal tissue) achieved both high "
         "malignant coverage and low predicted normal-tissue escape. The 10 best-ranked exploratory "
         "activator/blocker combinations still retain a large activator-positive / blocker-negative "
         "fraction in normal tissue (worst extra-prostatic co-detection shown, with donor support), so "
         "none is an attractive candidate; the blocker-negative call also depends on trusting scRNA "
         "dropout. This is reported as a negative result:\n\n" +
         nt.to_markdown(index=False))
```

# Limitations

- **Label leakage from the malignant annotation.** The discovery cohort defines malignant cells partly
  from prostate-cancer expression signatures (Liu, Wallace) that contain HPN and EPCAM. Pairs with those
  genes are excluded as confounded, and a de-leaked sensitivity (Robustness) shows the clean lead survives
  a malignant compartment defined without the panel genes. That check is partial; a complete fix would
  recompute the authors' annotation with all screened genes removed and a second CNV method.
- **Transcript co-detection is a selectivity surrogate, not therapeutic safety.** scRNA measures mRNA,
  not surface antigen density, extracellular epitope availability, binding affinity, shedding,
  internalization, the minimum antigen density for activation, or residual single-arm signalling. HPA
  localization supports membrane-localization plausibility but does not qualify targetability; same-cell
  surface co-expression needs CITE-seq, multiplex IHC, or dual-color flow to confirm.
- **Statistical uncertainty is quantified for the lead, not the whole table.** The nominated pair now
  carries bootstrap-over-patient intervals for coverage, a Pareto-membership probability, a donor-level
  95% upper bound for its worst liability, and a paired malignant-benign delta. A full hierarchical
  (beta-binomial mixed) model over all 325 pairs, with the selection re-run inside each bootstrap, remains
  future work, so the ranking of the non-lead pairs is still point estimates.
- **Assay-matched but single-assay.** The healthy reference is restricted to 10x 3' v3 to match the
  tumor, so Smart-seq2 and other-chemistry cells in Tabula Sapiens are excluded and a liability seen only
  on another platform would be missed. Dissociation also selectively loses fragile populations.
- **NOT-gate calls trust B-negatives**, which are dropout-sensitive, and none cleared the liability bar
  (a negative result).
- **One cohort, localised disease.** The discovery cohort is hormone-naive localised prostate cancer;
  replication in an independent and a castration-resistant / neuroendocrine cohort is future work.
- **Malignant labels are the authors' CNV/signature calls**, adopted rather than re-derived here.
- **The PSMA-PSCA benchmark is preclinical.** It is a safety/selectivity benchmark, not a coverage
  benchmark; its low co-detection is consistent with limited PSCA transcript detection in this dataset.

# Conclusion

This analysis is a patient-aware, transcriptome-based prioritization of candidate combinatorial antigens
in localized prostate cancer. The PSMA-PSCA benchmark shows the expected collapse in normal-tissue
transcript co-detection relative to its single markers. A single clean pair is nominated, PSMA x STEAP1:
higher malignant co-detection than the benchmark and, once the leakage-confounded HPN pairs are removed,
Pareto-non-dominated among the surface-accessible candidates. The target pair is already in clinical
development as an OR-like dual antibody-drug conjugate (ABBV-969), so the contribution is the AND framing,
not the pair. This is a hypothesis-generating ranking, not evidence of therapeutic safety. Independent
advanced-disease cohorts, protein-level same-cell measurement (CITE-seq, multiplex IHC) in the tumor and
its worst normal tissues, and recomputed leakage-free labels are required before it can be called a
validated target.

# Reproducibility

The pipeline runs from a clean clone with `uv`; every table in `results/tables/` is regenerated by the
numbered scripts in `scripts/`, and this report redraws its figures from those tables. See the README for
the exact command sequence.

# References

::: {#refs}
:::
